context: Tests whether the agent correctly treats private functions with docstrings as in-scope (auto-write), preserves existing docstring style when making targeted updates, excludes low-confidence prose README mentions from proposals, and does not add extra content (examples, notes) to updated docstrings.

checklist.name: Private symbol auto-updated
checklist.description: _parse_config docstring is auto-updated (written) despite being a private function, because it already has a docstring

checklist.name: timeout param added to docstring
checklist.description: The updated _parse_config docstring includes a description for the new 'timeout' parameter

checklist.name: Existing params preserved in order
checklist.description: The existing 'source' and 'strict' parameter descriptions appear before 'timeout' in the updated docstring (no reordering)

checklist.name: Note section untouched
checklist.description: The 'Note: See project wiki for key reference.' section is NOT removed or modified in the updated _parse_config docstring

checklist.name: No new sections added
checklist.description: No new 'Example', 'Raises', or 'See Also' sections are added to _parse_config's docstring

checklist.name: Indentation style matched
checklist.description: The new 'timeout' entry uses the same indentation style as the existing 'source' and 'strict' entries in the Args block

checklist.name: connect return type updated
checklist.description: The connect() docstring Returns section is updated to reflect Optional[socket.socket] (or that None may be returned)

checklist.name: connect Example unchanged
checklist.description: The 'Example:' block in connect's docstring is NOT modified even though the return type changed

checklist.name: Prose mention not proposed
checklist.description: The README prose mention 'The connect function accepts a host...' (no backtick) does NOT generate a README proposal — it is skipped or noted as low confidence

checklist.name: README backtick proposed
checklist.description: The backtick mention `connect` in README is identified as a candidate section for a proposed README update

checklist.name: README not auto-written
checklist.description: README.md file is not modified on disk — any README updates are proposals only
